# Lua-Yar

> Yar RPC protocol implementation for Lua. Zero-dependency, coroutine-friendly, binary protocol, interoperable with PHP Yar / yar-C / lua-resty-yar. Supports TCP / HTTP / Unix Socket transport, runs on standard Lua + luasocket, OpenResty, copas, lua-eco, Skynet.

Lua-Yar is a pure Lua implementation of the [Yar](https://github.com/laruence/yar) RPC framework (Yet Another RPC Framework), originally from the PHP ecosystem. It enables cross-language RPC via binary protocol streams, with no IDL compilation or heavy runtime required.

## Quick Start

```lua
local Yar = require("yar")

-- Client
local client = Yar.client.new("tcp://127.0.0.1:8888")
local result = client:call("add", {1, 2})  -- returns 3

-- Server
local server = Yar.server.new({
    add = function(a, b) return a + b end,
})
server:listen("tcp://0.0.0.0:8888")
server:loop()
```

Install: `luarocks install lua-yar`

## Docs

- [Protocol Specification](docs/protocol.md): YAR binary protocol format — header, framing, packager, request/response body
- [API Reference](docs/api.md): Server / Client / Packager / Transport API signatures and usage
- [Server Implementation Examples](docs/server-implementations.md): OpenResty / copas / lua-eco / Skynet / coroutine integration patterns
- [Tutorial](docs/tutorial.md): Step-by-step guide from first client call to production server
- [How-To Guide](docs/how-to.md): Common tasks — custom packager, HTTP provider injection, hooks, TLS, unix socket
- [Design Rationale](docs/design-rationale.md): Architecture decisions, error handling philosophy, OOP patterns

## Examples

- [TCP Server](example/server_tcp.lua): minimal TCP server with loop()
- [HTTP Server](example/server_http.lua): HTTP server with luasocket
- [OpenResty Server](example/server_openresty.lua): ngx.content_by_lua integration
- [Copas Server](example/server_copas.lua): copas coroutine concurrent server
- [lua-eco Server](example/server_luaeco.lua): lua-eco integration
- [Client](example/client.lua): basic client usage
- [Hooks](example/hooks.lua): request/response interception
- [Custom C Extension Packager](example/register_cext.lua): register cjson/cmsgpack as packager

## Design

- [Design Decisions (ADR Index)](docs/design/decisions.md): 32 architecture decision records across 7 modules
- [Transport Layer](docs/design/transport-layer.md): socket abstraction, HTTP delegation, connection management
- [Server Layer](docs/design/server-layer.md): Dispatcher / Transport / Daemon three-layer separation
- [Protocol Layer](docs/design/protocol-layer.md): framing, header, packager protocol compliance
- [Cross-Cutting Concerns](docs/design/cross-cutting.md): error handling, logging, hooks, naming conventions
